steps.title: Check shape first
steps.content: The shown line of best fit rises from left to right and passes close to the origin. That means the model should have a positive slope and a $y$-intercept near $0$.

steps.title: Eliminate by graph behavior
steps.content: Choices C and D are horizontal lines, so they do not model an upward trend. Choice B has negative slope, so it would fall from left to right. Only choice A has a positive slope and passes through the origin.

steps.title: Use Desmos to compare choices
steps.content: Graph the four choices and compare them with the shown line of best fit. The line $y=1.1x$ has the correct direction and intercept, so it best matches the scatterplot.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The best-fit equation is $y=1.1x$, which matches choice A.
